qa_pairs.question: What is the mandatory initial step for PIs and sponsored users to access any computational resource offered by the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: They must first request access to the desired system(s) on the Alliance CCDB page: https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_systems.

qa_pairs.question: Why might a user be unable to log in to a desired Alliance computational system?
qa_pairs.answer: Users will not be able to log in if they miss the step of requesting access to the desired system(s) or accepting additional agreements (e.g., Terms of Use, SLAs).

qa_pairs.question: When do PIs and their sponsored users gain access to default storage resources?
qa_pairs.answer: Default storage resources are made available immediately after creating an Alliance account on CCDB and requesting access to the corresponding system.

qa_pairs.question: What are the maximum limits for additional project and nearline storage that PIs can request via RAS without a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs can request up to a maximum of 40 TB of project storage and 100 TB of nearline storage across all clusters combined.

qa_pairs.question: How do PIs request additional storage resources via RAS?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs (not sponsored users) must send an email to support@tech.alliancecan.ca with details of the storage resources needed.

qa_pairs.question: Which HPC clusters do not offer RAS storage allocations?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium and HPSS do not offer RAS storage.

qa_pairs.question: How are CPU resources generally provided to research groups with an active Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: CPU resources are available for 'opportunistic use' to all research groups with an active Alliance account.

qa_pairs.question: What is the average CPU resource target for research groups utilizing their Default RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: Research groups may be able to use, on average, up to 200 core years on each cluster with their Default RAP.

qa_pairs.question: When might applying for a RAC be a more suitable option for CPU resources than opportunistic use?
qa_pairs.answer: Applying for a RAC may be the best option for jobs with large memory requirements or if the amount of CPU resources needed exceeds the minimum required to apply for RAC.

qa_pairs.question: What is the availability status of GPU resources for opportunistic use?
qa_pairs.answer: GPU resources are available for opportunistic use, but their availability varies greatly and becomes more limited in periods preceding major conferences due to high demand.

qa_pairs.question: Does the Alliance guarantee a specific amount of GPU resources for opportunistic use?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the Alliance cannot guarantee any amount of GPU resources available for opportunistic use, especially during times of high demand.

qa_pairs.question: How can PIs request modest amounts of cloud resources via RAS?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs must complete a specific Google Form with details about the cloud resources needed.

qa_pairs.question: Who should users contact if they have questions about accessing RAS cloud resources or require assistance?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should contact cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca for questions or help regarding RAS cloud resources.
